title: galium pusillum subsp. idubedae
intro: Galium pusillum subsp. idubedae, also known as the small bedstraw, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to certain parts of North Africa and the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical creams, and has been associated with health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting skin health.
